title: What if the real AI infrastructure trade is actually copper?
author: EpochSasquatch
selftext:
Everyone talks about semiconductors when discussing AI.



But AI infrastructure also needs:



*  power grids
*  transformers
*  transmission lines
*  backup systems
* cooling systems
*  industrial construction



All of that requires huge amounts of copper.



The interesting part is that supply growth still looks constrained globally while permitting timelines for major new mines can stretch well beyond 10 years.



That is why I think some smaller copper exploration companies are beginning to attract more attention again.



NovaRed is interesting to me because the company appears to be combining several trends into one story:



*  BC copper exposure
*  district-scale land position
*  AI-assisted exploration systems
*  infrastructure-finance experience



Its Wilmac project spans roughly 16,078 hectares near existing Copper Mountain infrastructure in British Columbia.



At the same time, the company has been developing MetalCore, which appears focused on integrating AI, geological datasets and mineral intelligence into exploration targeting.



Whether that works commercially remains unknown, but it is at least different from the standard junior mining approach.



The market cap is still very small relative to most public copper companies, which makes the risk high but also explains why some speculative investors are watching it closely.



Feels like copper may become one of the more important macro stories of the next decade.
